causes.cause: Calling a method on an uninitialised or null object reference
causes.recommendation: Add an explicit null-check before dereferencing

causes.cause: Returning null from a method that callers do not guard against
causes.recommendation: Return Optional<T> instead of raw null

causes.cause: Uninitialised field accessed before the constructor completes
causes.recommendation: Initialise all fields in the constructor or with field defaults

causes.cause: Accessing an attribute on a None object
causes.recommendation: Guard with: if obj is not None: obj.attribute
causes.code_example:
if user is not None:
    name = user.name

causes.cause: Typo in the attribute name
causes.recommendation: Use dir(obj) or IDE auto-complete to verify the correct name

causes.cause: Object type does not have the expected attribute at runtime
causes.recommendation: Use hasattr(obj, 'attr') before access, or getattr(obj, 'attr', default)

causes.cause: Performing an operation on incompatible data types
causes.recommendation: Ensure explicit type conversion before the operation

causes.cause: Calling a function with wrong number or type of arguments
causes.recommendation: Check the function signature and match the call parameters

causes.cause: Treating a non-callable object as a function
causes.recommendation: Verify the variable holds a callable before invoking it

causes.cause: Passing a value that is the right type but semantically invalid (e.g. int('abc'))
causes.recommendation: Validate input before conversion using try/except or a regex check

causes.cause: Unpacking a sequence into the wrong number of variables
causes.recommendation: Ensure the sequence length matches the number of unpack targets
causes.code_example: a, b, c = some_list  # raises if len(some_list) != 3

causes.cause: Providing an out-of-range argument to a maths or conversion function
causes.recommendation: Clamp or validate values before passing to math/conversion functions

causes.cause: Dictionary changed size during iteration
causes.recommendation: Iterate over a copy: for k in list(d): ...
causes.code_example:
for key in list(dictionary.keys()):
    del dictionary[key]

causes.cause: Generator raised StopIteration inside a generator body (Python 3.7+ RuntimeError)
causes.recommendation: Replace bare StopIteration raises with a return statement inside generators

causes.cause: asyncio coroutine called outside a running event loop
causes.recommendation: Use asyncio.run() at the top level or await inside an async context

causes.cause: Missing or incorrect base case in a recursive function
causes.recommendation: Add a terminating base case and verify it is reached for all inputs

causes.cause: Accidental mutual or self-recursion in dunder methods (__repr__, __eq__)
causes.recommendation: Check __repr__, __str__, __eq__ for calls that trigger themselves

causes.cause: OS file/directory permissions deny read, write, or execute access
causes.recommendation: Run chmod/chown to fix permissions, or run the process as the correct user

causes.cause: Attempting to write to a read-only filesystem or protected system directory
causes.recommendation: Write to a user-writable path (e.g. /tmp or user home directory)

causes.cause: Remote service or database did not respond within the configured timeout
causes.recommendation: Increase timeout threshold and add retry with exponential back-off

causes.cause: Slow query or CPU-bound task blocking the event loop
causes.recommendation: Move heavy work to a thread-pool executor or a background worker

causes.cause: Network congestion or packet loss causing connection delay
causes.recommendation: Check network health and implement the circuit-breaker pattern

causes.cause: Missing closing bracket, parenthesis, or quotation mark
causes.recommendation: Use an editor with bracket matching to locate the unclosed delimiter

causes.cause: Reserved keyword used as a variable or function name
causes.recommendation: Rename the identifier to avoid collision with a language keyword

causes.cause: Variable referenced before it is assigned in the current scope
causes.recommendation: Ensure the variable is assigned before first use in all code paths

causes.cause: Typo in the variable name
causes.recommendation: Check spelling; use IDE auto-complete to avoid typos

causes.cause: Variable defined inside a conditional block and used outside when the condition was false
causes.recommendation: Initialise the variable with a default value before the conditional block

causes.cause: Reading a file opened with the wrong encoding (e.g. ASCII vs UTF-8)
causes.recommendation: Open with explicit encoding: open(path, encoding='utf-8')
causes.code_example:
with open('file.txt', encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') as f:
    content = f.read()

causes.cause: Binary HTTP response not decoded before text processing
causes.recommendation: Use response.text instead of response.content, or decode manually

causes.cause: File contains mixed or legacy encoding (Latin-1, Windows-1252)
causes.recommendation: Detect encoding with chardet, then open with the detected codec

causes.cause: Denominator is zero with no guard at the call site
causes.recommendation: Check divisor before division
causes.code_example: result = numerator / denominator if denominator != 0 else 0

causes.cause: User-supplied input accepted without validation and used directly as a divisor
causes.recommendation: Validate and sanitise user inputs; reject zero values explicitly

causes.cause: Infinitely recursive call — base case missing or unreachable
causes.recommendation: Add a base case or convert the recursion to an iterative loop

causes.cause: Circular object serialisation (e.g. JSON.stringify on a circular structure)
causes.recommendation: Break circular references or use a replacer function in JSON.stringify

causes.cause: JAR or class file absent from the runtime classpath
causes.recommendation: Add the missing dependency to pom.xml / build.gradle

causes.cause: Fully-qualified class name passed to Class.forName() contains a typo
causes.recommendation: Verify the class name string including the full package path

causes.cause: Method received an argument that violates its documented contract
causes.recommendation: Read the method Javadoc for preconditions and validate before calling

causes.cause: Negative value passed where only positive is allowed
causes.recommendation: Add input validation: Preconditions.checkArgument(n > 0) or a manual if-throw

causes.cause: Heap space exhausted by unbounded object creation or loading a large dataset at once
causes.recommendation: Increase -Xmx JVM flag or switch to streaming / pagination

causes.cause: Memory leak — objects held in static collections or caches without eviction
causes.recommendation: Use WeakReference, SoftReference, or a cache with an eviction policy (Caffeine, Guava)

causes.cause: Metaspace exhausted by classloader leaks (dynamic class generation)
causes.recommendation: Increase -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize and profile classloaders for leaks

causes.cause: Using an undeclared variable — var/let/const declaration is missing
causes.recommendation: Declare all variables with let or const before use

causes.cause: Variable accessed inside the temporal dead zone (TDZ) of a let/const declaration
causes.recommendation: Move the let/const declaration above the first point of use

causes.cause: Creating an Array with a negative, non-finite, or non-integer length
causes.recommendation: Validate the value before passing it to new Array(n)

causes.cause: Number.toFixed() or toPrecision() called with an out-of-range argument
causes.recommendation: Clamp precision to the valid range [0, 100] before calling

causes.cause: await is missing — Promise rejection propagates without being caught
causes.recommendation: Wrap async calls in try/catch or add .catch() to the Promise chain

causes.cause: Error thrown inside a Promise executor without calling reject()
causes.recommendation: Always call reject(err) inside the catch branch of Promise executors

causes.cause: Event handler returns a Promise that is not awaited by the calling framework
causes.recommendation: Make the event handler async and await all inner Promises

causes.cause: Dereferencing a null or uninitialised pointer
causes.recommendation: Initialise all pointers to NULL and check before dereferencing

causes.cause: Heap buffer overflow — writing past the end of a malloc'd block
causes.recommendation: Use bounds-checked functions (strncpy, snprintf) and validate sizes before writes

causes.cause: Stack buffer overflow from a large VLA or deep recursion exceeding stack size
causes.recommendation: Use heap allocation for large buffers; limit recursion depth

causes.cause: Target service is not running or is bound to a different port
causes.recommendation: Start the service and confirm the correct host:port

causes.cause: Firewall or security group rule blocking the outbound connection
causes.recommendation: Check iptables / cloud security-group rules; open the required port

causes.cause: Incorrect host or port in the application configuration
causes.recommendation: Verify host:port in config file or environment variable

causes.cause: Package is not installed in the active Python environment
causes.recommendation: pip install <package> or reinstall from requirements.txt

causes.cause: Circular import between two modules
causes.recommendation: Move the import inside the function body or extract a shared module

causes.cause: Module directory is not on sys.path / PYTHONPATH
causes.recommendation: Activate the virtual environment or add the project root to PYTHONPATH

causes.cause: Package name typo in the import statement
causes.recommendation: Check the exact package name on PyPI and correct the import

causes.cause: Package installed in a different Python environment than the one running the script
causes.recommendation: Run 'which python' and 'pip show <package>' to confirm the active environment

causes.cause: Loading an entire large file or dataset into memory at once
causes.recommendation: Stream or chunk the data: pandas read_csv(chunksize=) or Python generators

causes.cause: Memory leak — objects accumulating in collections without being freed
causes.recommendation: Profile with tracemalloc or memory_profiler to locate the leak

causes.cause: Producing an extremely large intermediate data structure (Cartesian product, etc.)
causes.recommendation: Replace with lazy iterators (itertools) to avoid materialising huge structures

causes.cause: Index out of range — accessing a position beyond the list/array length
causes.recommendation: Bounds-check before indexing

causes.cause: Simultaneously iterating and deleting elements, shifting remaining indices
causes.recommendation: Iterate over a copy or use list comprehension instead

causes.cause: Accessing a key that does not exist in the dictionary
causes.recommendation: Use dict.get('key', default) or check 'key' in dict first

causes.cause: Key name typo or case-sensitivity mismatch
causes.recommendation: Print dict.keys() at runtime to verify available keys

causes.cause: File path is wrong or the file has not been created yet
causes.recommendation: Use Path.exists() before opening and log the resolved absolute path

causes.cause: Relative path resolves differently depending on the working directory
causes.recommendation: Use Path(__file__).parent / 'relative/path' for script-relative paths

causes.cause: File was deleted or moved by another process during execution
causes.recommendation: Use file locking or re-check existence inside the critical section

causes.cause: HTTP response is not JSON (HTML error page or empty body returned instead)
causes.recommendation: Check response.status_code and Content-Type before calling .json()

causes.cause: JSON file has a trailing comma or JavaScript-style comments — both are invalid JSON
causes.recommendation: Validate with a JSON linter; use commentjson library for commented config files

causes.cause: Connection string is wrong or database server is unreachable
causes.recommendation: Verify DATABASE_URL and check that the DB server is running

causes.cause: Schema mismatch — column or table does not exist because migration was not run
causes.recommendation: Run database migrations: alembic upgrade head (or equivalent)

causes.cause: Connection pool exhausted by too many concurrent queries
causes.recommendation: Increase pool_size in SQLAlchemy or set a connection limit on the DB server

causes.cause: Unwrapping a None/Err value with .unwrap() in Rust, or nil pointer dereference in Go
causes.recommendation: Use pattern matching (if let / match) in Rust; nil-check before use in Go

causes.cause: Slice/array index out of bounds at runtime
causes.recommendation: Bounds-check before indexing, or use .get() which returns Option in Rust

causes.cause: API request body does not conform to the expected Pydantic / JSON schema
causes.recommendation: Read the validation error detail to find exactly which field failed and why

causes.cause: Enum or Literal field receives a value not declared in the schema
causes.recommendation: Update the Enum definition or sanitise the value upstream

causes.cause: A nested model has a required field missing from the incoming payload
causes.recommendation: Make the field Optional with a default, or ensure the upstream service sends it

categories.invalid_value: Correct type but semantically invalid value
categories.undefined_variable: Using variables before declaration or out of scope
categories.runtime_state: Error caused by inconsistent program state at runtime
categories.network: Network connectivity and service availability issues
categories.resource: System resource exhaustion (memory, file descriptors, thread pool)
categories.array_access: Invalid array, list, or slice indexing
categories.file_system: File system access, path resolution, and permission issues
categories.memory_access: Invalid memory read/write in native languages
categories.async_error: Errors in asynchronous or Promise-based code
categories.database: Database connectivity, schema, and query errors
